'''Grant William Millington''' (born 1 November 1986) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who last played as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Western Suburbs Red Devils]] in the [[Country Rugby League]].

He previously played for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] and in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] between 2008 and 2010, and for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in 2011.<ref name=":0">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/15671940|title=Castleford Tigers sign Grant Millington & Lee Mitchell|date=10 November 2011|work=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=10 November 2011}}</ref>

Millington holds the Castleford club record for most appearances made by an overseas player, accumulating 244 across his 10 seasons with the Tigers.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|date=2021-08-27|title=Grant Millington to retire|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/35934/grant-millington-to-retire|website=Rugby-League.com}}</ref> He is widely recognised as one of the club's greatest imports, and is considered a fans' favourite due to his consistency and commitment as well as his community contributions off the pitch.<ref name=":1"/><ref name=":2">{{Cite news|last=Craven|first=Dave|date=2021-08-27|title=Castleford Tigers veteran Grant Millington to call time on his rugby career|work=The Yorkshire Post|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-veteran-grant-millington-to-call-time-on-his-rugby-career-3362793|access-date=2022-01-04}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Gordon|first=James|date=2021-10-18|title=Grant Millington makes touching farewell gesture to Castleford|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/grant-millington-makes-touching-farewell-gesture-to-castleford/|access-date=2022-01-04|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He was utilised as a skilful, ball-handling and intelligent prop/second-row forward and was even called upon to cover in the halves on occasion.<ref name=":1"/><ref name=":2"/>

==Early life==
Millington was born in [[Auburn, New South Wales|Auburn]], New South Wales, Australia.

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Cabramatta Two Blues]] and [[Wyong Roos]].

==Playing career==
=== Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks ===
Millington made his first grade debut for Cronulla in round 10 of the [[2008 NRL season]] against Brisbane. Cronulla-Sutherland went on to finish third in 2008 but Millington made no appearances for the club during the finals series. 
[[File:Grant Millington 2008.jpg|thumb|right|Millington being tackled by [[James Maloney (rugby league)|James Maloney]] in 2008]]

In the [[2009 NRL season]], Millington made 17 appearances for Cronulla-Sutherland as they finished 2nd last on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2009/cronulla/summary.html/|title=Cronulla 2009 season|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>

In the [[2010 NRL season]], Millington played in nearly every game for Cronulla-Sutherland that year as the club finished near the bottom of the ladder.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2010/cronulla/summary.html/|title=Cronulla 2010 season|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>

=== Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs ===
In 2011, Millington joined Canterbury-Bankstown but only managed to make seven appearances before being released by the club. After Canterbury-Bankstown failed to make the NRL finals series in 2011 they agreed to release him from the remainder of his playing contract to allow him to take up a two-year deal with the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League (Europe)|European Super League]].<ref name="Bulldogs Release Castleford-bound Grant Millington">{{cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/bulldogs-release-castleford-bound-grant-millington/|title=Bulldogs Release Castleford-bound Grant Millington|publisher=League Unlimited|date=|access-date=1 April 2021}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
Ahead of the 2012 season, Millington joined English side Castleford on a two-year deal.<ref name=":0" /> He had an instant impact, making 22 appearances and scoring 4 tries in his first season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/player.php?id=11|title=Grant Millington|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-16}}</ref>

In July 2013, Millington signed a one-year contract extension with the Tigers, with head coach [[Daryl Powell]] labelling him "a stand-out performer".<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23199499|title=Grant Millington signs new Castleford Tigers contract|date=2013-07-05|access-date=2019-06-16|language=en-GB}}</ref>

This was followed by a further contract extension in February 2014.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/26261437|title=Grant Millington extends Castleford Tigers deal|date=2014-02-20|access-date=2019-06-16|language=en-GB}}</ref> Millington was voted as the Fan's Player of the Year for the 2014 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.castigers.com/article.php?id=4736|title=2014 Player of the Year Awards Winners|last=|first=|date=|website=|access-date=}}</ref>

Millington was first given the number 10 shirt for 2015.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Bower|first=Aaron|date=2015-01-07|title=Castleford Tigers unveil 2015 squad numbers|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-unveil-2015-squad-numbers/|access-date=2022-01-04|website=Total Rugby League}}</ref> He was named Directors' Player of the Year and 2nd Place Player of the Year for the 2015 season, of which he played every game.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=4372|title=Player of the Year 2015|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-16}}</ref>

In 2016, due to the Tigers' extensive injury list, Millington displayed his positional versatility by starting 3 games in the halves for Castleford.

Millington played a crucial role in the 2017 Castleford campaign, contributing to the Tigers' first [[League Leaders' Shield]] and [[Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] appearance. He played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat to the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref> He was consequently named in the 2017 [[Super League Dream Team]],<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41387829|title=Super League Dream Team: Six Castleford Tigers players in team of 2017 season|date=2017-09-25|access-date=2019-06-16|language=en-GB}}</ref> and won the club's 2nd Place Player of the Year award.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5649|title=2017 Winners|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-16}}</ref>

Millington was named 3rd Place Player of the Year for the 2018 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6122|title=2018 Awards Winners|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-16}}</ref>

At the Castleford Tigers 2019 end-of-season awards night, Millington picked up the Community Player of the Year title in recognition of his outstanding contribution to the local area and his hard work off the field in conjunction with the Castleford Tigers Community Foundation.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6587|title=Watts at the treble on awards night|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-11}}</ref>

Following his excellent start to the 2020 Super League season, Castleford head coach Powell stated that "this year he's at a different level" and expressed his intention to open further contract talks with Millington.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/grant-millington-stakes-claim-make-it-decade-castleford-tigers-2003360|title=Grant Millington stakes claim to make it a decade at Castleford Tigers|website=www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2020-04-11}}</ref> He signed a contract in October 2020 to take him through to the conclusion of the [[2021 Super League season]].<ref name="'I feel like I'm flying' - Castleford Tigers stalwart Grant Millington not ready to call it quits">{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/i-feel-im-flying-castleford-tigers-stalwart-grant-millington-not-ready-call-it-quits-3137074|title='I feel like I'm flying' - Castleford Tigers stalwart Grant Millington not ready to call it quits|publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=17 February 2021|access-date=1 April 2021}}</ref><ref name="Grant Millington determined to give Daryl Powell one last roar ahead of Castleford Tigers exit">{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/grant-millington-daryl-powell-castleford-20272409|title=Grant Millington determined to give Daryl Powell one last roar ahead of Castleford Tigers exit|publisher=Yorkshire Live|date=28 March 2021|access-date=1 April 2021}}</ref>

On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> In August 2021, Millington announced that he would be retiring at the end of the regular season.<ref name=":1"/> He scored a try in his final appearance for Castleford, in a 24-38 defeat to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[The Jungle (Wheldon Road)|Wheldon Road]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-09-16|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 24-40 Warrington Wolves - Tigers play-off hopes over after loss|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58573945|access-date=2022-01-04|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
